marketing.cgv.title: General Terms and Conditions of Sale (CGV)
marketing.cgv.section1.title: Scope of Application
marketing.cgv.section1.body:
These General Terms and Conditions of Sale (hereinafter 'CGV') govern all contractual relationships between Epic Shop, a simplified joint-stock company with a capital of €10,000, registered with the Paris Trade and Companies Register under number 123 456 789, whose registered office is located at 1 rue de la Paix, 75002 Paris, France (hereinafter the 'Seller'), and any natural or legal person making a purchase on the website www.epic-shop.fr (hereinafter the 'Customer').

The CGV apply to all orders placed on the Site. Placing an order implies the Customer's full and unreserved acceptance of these CGV, to the exclusion of any other document such as brochures, catalogues, or the Customer's own purchasing terms.

The Seller reserves the right to modify the CGV at any time. The applicable CGV are those in force on the day the order is placed. Any order placed after new CGV are published constitutes acceptance of the latter.
marketing.cgv.section2.title: Products
marketing.cgv.section2.body:
The products offered for sale are those appearing on the Site on the day of consultation by the Customer, subject to available stock. Each product page includes a description, photographs as faithful as possible, the sale price, and the essential characteristics of the product in accordance with Article L. 111-1 of the French Consumer Code.

Product photographs and visuals are provided for illustrative purposes only and have no contractual value. The Seller shall not be held liable for any errors or omissions in the descriptions.

In the event a product is unavailable after an order is placed, the Seller shall inform the Customer as soon as possible and shall refund all amounts paid within a maximum of fourteen (14) days from the date the Customer is informed.
marketing.cgv.section3.title: Prices
marketing.cgv.section3.body:
Product sale prices are indicated in euros (€) and include all taxes (TTC), excluding delivery charges. The applicable VAT rate is the French rate in force on the day of the order (standard 20% VAT by default, which may vary depending on the nature of the product and the country of delivery for intra-community shipments).

Delivery charges are calculated based on weight, package dimensions, and destination, and are clearly indicated to the Customer before final order confirmation.

The Seller reserves the right to modify prices at any time. However, the price invoiced to the Customer is that in force at the time of order confirmation. In the event of a VAT rate change between order and invoicing, the new rate will be applied and reflected in the TTC price.
marketing.cgv.section4.title: Payment
marketing.cgv.section4.body:
Payment for orders is made exclusively online by credit card (Carte Bleue, Visa, MasterCard, American Express) via the secure payment provider Stripe. The card is debited at the time of order confirmation.

The Customer guarantees that they have the necessary authorisations to use the chosen payment method when placing an order. The Seller reserves the right to suspend or cancel any order in the event of suspected fraud or payment default.

Payment data is collected and processed directly by Stripe, in compliance with the PCI-DSS standard. The Seller does not retain any sensitive banking data. An electronic invoice is issued for each order and made available to the Customer in their personal account area.
marketing.cgv.section5.title: Delivery
marketing.cgv.section5.body:
Products are delivered to the address indicated by the Customer at the time of ordering. Delivery times are indicated on the product page and confirmed during the ordering process. They are expressed in working days and run from the date of order confirmation by the Seller.

If the delivery date is exceeded by more than seven (7) days and is not due to force majeure, the Customer may request cancellation of the sale and a full refund under the conditions set out in Article L. 216-2 of the French Consumer Code.

The Customer is required to check the condition of the packaging and the product at the time of delivery. In the event of damage, a damaged parcel, or a missing product, the Customer must lodge precise and detailed reservations with the carrier and inform the Seller within forty-eight (48) hours. No claim will be accepted after this deadline.
marketing.cgv.section6.title: Right of Withdrawal
marketing.cgv.section6.body:
In accordance with Articles L. 221-18 et seq. of the French Consumer Code, the Customer has a period of fourteen (14) calendar days from receipt of the product to exercise their right of withdrawal, without having to justify their decision or bear any costs other than those provided for in Articles L. 221-23 to L. 221-25.

To exercise this right, the Customer must notify the Seller of their decision to withdraw by email to support@epic-shop.fr or by post to the registered office address. The Customer may also use the standard withdrawal form available on the Site.

The product must be returned in its original packaging, in perfect condition, complete and accompanied by all its accessories, within fourteen (14) days following notification of the withdrawal decision. Direct return costs are borne by the Customer, unless the product cannot be returned by post due to its nature.

The Seller shall refund all amounts paid, including standard delivery costs, within fourteen (14) days of recovering the product or receiving proof of shipment from the Customer. The refund shall be made using the same payment method used for the order, unless the Customer expressly agrees otherwise.

Excluded from the right of withdrawal, in accordance with Article L. 221-28: products made to the Customer's specifications or clearly personalised, perishable products, unsealed audio/video recordings or software, and unsealed hygiene products.
marketing.cgv.section7.title: Legal Warranties
marketing.cgv.section7.body:
All products sold by the Seller are covered by the legal warranties provided for by the French Consumer Code and Civil Code:

• Legal warranty of conformity (Articles L. 217-1 to L. 217-14 of the Consumer Code): the Customer has a period of two (2) years from delivery of the goods to bring an action under the conformity warranty. In the event of a lack of conformity, the Customer may choose between repair or replacement of the goods, subject to the cost conditions set out in Article L. 217-9. The conformity warranty applies regardless of any commercial warranty that may have been taken out.

• Legal warranty against hidden defects (Articles 1641 to 1649 of the Civil Code): the Customer may bring an action under the hidden defects warranty within two (2) years of discovering the defect. In the event of a hidden defect, the Customer may choose between cancellation of the sale or a price reduction.

For any claim under these warranties, the Customer must contact Customer Service at support@epic-shop.fr, describing the defect in detail and attaching photographs where possible. The Seller undertakes to process all claims as soon as possible.
marketing.cgv.section8.title: Liability
marketing.cgv.section8.body:
The Seller undertakes to perform its obligations with all due diligence. The Seller's liability shall not be engaged in the event of force majeure or an unforeseeable and irresistible act of a third party to the contract, within the meaning of Article 1218 of the French Civil Code and the case law of the Court of Cassation.

The Seller shall not be held liable for indirect or intangible damages such as loss of revenue, loss of profits, loss of data, reputational damage, or any other financial or commercial loss resulting from use of the Site or purchase of products.

The Seller implements all reasonable means to ensure the availability and security of the Site. However, the Seller shall not be held liable for temporary interruptions of the Site for maintenance operations, technical issues, or force majeure events.
marketing.cgv.section9.title: Personal Data Protection (GDPR)
marketing.cgv.section9.body:
The Seller collects and processes the Customer's personal data in accordance with the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR — EU Regulation 2016/679) and the amended French Data Protection Act of 6 January 1978.

The data collected is strictly necessary for order processing (identity, delivery address, email address, phone number) and for managing the commercial relationship. Banking data is collected and processed exclusively by Stripe, a PCI-DSS certified provider.

The Customer has the right to access, rectify, erase, restrict processing, data portability, and object to the processing of their data. To exercise these rights, the Customer may contact the Data Protection Officer at dpo@epic-shop.fr.

The Customer also has the right to lodge a complaint with the French Data Protection Authority (CNIL — www.cnil.fr) if they believe that their data processing does not comply with the regulations.

The detailed privacy policy is available at /privacy and forms an integral part of these CGV.
marketing.cgv.section10.title: Applicable Law and Dispute Resolution
marketing.cgv.section10.body:
These CGV are governed by French law. The language of the CGV and the contract is French.

In the event of a dispute, the Customer shall first contact the Seller's Customer Service at support@epic-shop.fr to seek an amicable solution.

In accordance with Articles L. 611-1 et seq. of the French Consumer Code, the Customer has the right to use a consumer mediator free of charge. The competent mediator is the FEVAD e-commerce Mediator (French E-commerce and Distance Selling Federation), reachable via the European Commission's Online Dispute Resolution platform: https://ec.europa.eu/consumers/odr.

Failing an amicable resolution, any dispute relating to the interpretation, performance, or termination of these CGV shall be submitted to the competent courts within the jurisdiction of the Paris Court of Appeal, notwithstanding multiple defendants or third-party claims.
